items.title: Kenya: Witia Expands Digital Education Drive With Second Smart Library
items.blurb: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Women in Technology and Innovation Africa (WITIA) has expanded its digital education programme with the launch of its second Smart Library in Machakos County.

items.title: Somalia: Somali President Meets Italian Interior Minister to Boost Bilateral Cooperation
items.blurb: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somali President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud on Friday received a high-level Italian delegation led by Interior Minister Matteo Piantedosi for talks focused on strengthening bilateral relations and expanding cooperation on security, migration and state-building.

items.title: Somalia: Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger Begin Formal Withdrawal From ICC
items.blurb: [Shabelle] Ouagadougou -- Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger have formally launched the process of withdrawing from the International Criminal Court (ICC) after submitting official notifications to leave the Rome Statute, according to a statement from the Assembly of States Parties to the court.

items.title: Kenya: Supreme Court Halts Cytonn Property Transfers Pending Appeal
items.blurb: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- The Supreme Court has handed Cytonn Investments a temporary legal reprieve by suspending the implementation of Court of Appeal orders that had cleared the way for the Official Receiver to take control of and transfer several high-value properties linked to the firm's troubled real estate investment schemes.

items.title: Uganda: Lord Mayor Applauds Uganda Waragi's Infrastructure Boost At Port Bell Landing Site
items.blurb: [Independent (Kampala)] Kampala -- Uganda Breweries Limited (UBL), through its flagship brand Uganda Waragi, has commissioned a transformative community infrastructure project at Port Bell Landing Site in Luzira. As part of Uganda Waragi's landmark 60-year Diamond Jubilee legacy, the intervention delivers solar-powered flood lighting and structured waste management systems designed to eliminate public health hazards, protect livelihoods, and boost the night economy.

items.title: Uganda: Leaving, Returning, Surviving - the Climate Migration Dilemma in Bududa
items.blurb: [Independent (Kampala)] Bududa -- Peter Kibeti has returned to Kubewo village, Bududa district, a place he knows is dangerous and could kill him.

items.title: Malawi: Perfect Property Solutions Boosts M'mbelwa Royal Golf Tournament With K3.5 Million
items.blurb: [Nyasa Times] Perfect Property Solutions Company Limited has boosted the upcoming M'mbelwa Royal Golf Tournament with a K3.5 million sponsorship. The handover took place this past Friday ahead of the tournament, which is scheduled for Saturday at the Lilongwe Golf Club.

items.title: Malawi: Shanil's SIM Card 'Active After Her Death' - Family Demands Answers
items.blurb: [Nyasa Times] Edna Muluzi, daughter of crash victim Shanil Dzimbiri Muluzi, has appeared before the Parliamentary Ad Hoc Committee investigating the 10 June 2024 military plane crash that killed Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others -- and presented a list of 11 questions she says the committee must answer.

items.title: Malawi: Cira Calls for Strategic Reforms to Boost Construction Sector
items.blurb: [Nyasa Times] The Construction Industry Regulatory Authority (CIRA) has called for urgent corrective measures to address systemic challenges within the construction sector, asserting that such reforms are essential for the industry to achieve national and regional competitiveness.

items.title: Nigeria: Woof Studios Africa Highlights Creator Economy At Cannes Lions Festival
items.blurb: [Premium Times] One of the sessions wherein the delegation featured was titled "How Africa's Creators Are Building Culture as Infrastructure.

items.title: Nigeria: Abia, Anambra Commit to Peaceful Resolution of Boundary Disputes
items.blurb: [Premium Times] Officials from both states, representatives of the National Boundary Commission (NBC), traditional rulers, and other stakeholders held a joint meeting in Awka on Thursday to chart a path forward for resolving ongoing boundary issues.

items.title: Nigeria: Education Stakeholders in Ebonyi Reject the Proposed 6-3-3-4 Policy Reversal
items.blurb: [Premium Times] The stakeholders, who spoke separately with the News Agency of Nigeria on Friday in Abakaliki, described the proposed move as detrimental to Nigeria's effort to develop a functional and competitive education system.

items.title: Rwanda: What's On This Liberation Weekend?
items.blurb: [New Times] Liberation weekend brings one of the busiest entertainment stretches of the season, with concerts, theatre, worship gatherings and lakefront parties lined up in Kigali, Rubavu and Bugesera.

items.title: Rwanda: Gen Karuretwa Urges Young Rwandans to Be Active Contributors to Development
items.blurb: [New Times] Brig Gen Patrick Karuretwa, the Rwanda Defence Force (RDF) Spokesperson and Director General of International Military Cooperation, has called on young Rwandans to rethink patriotism and choose to be citizens who contribute to the country's development.

items.title: Rwanda: Govt Increases Fertiliser Subsidies Amid Global Price Shocks
items.blurb: [New Times] The government of Rwanda said it will increase subsidies on selected fertilisers for the 2027A planting season to mitigate the impact of a sharp rise in global input prices.

items.title: Cameroon: The Archbishop of Douala Denounces - 'Inhumane Prisons and a Corrupt Justice System'
items.blurb: [Agenzia Fides] Yaoundé -- Forced disappearances, arbitrary detention and inhumane prison conditions are among the grave abuses denounced by Archbishop Samuel Kleda of Douala in a pastoral Letter on prison conditions published at the end of June.

items.title: Kenya: Kindiki Defends Sale of Stake in Government Agencies to Fund Major Infrastructure Projects
items.blurb: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has defended the sale of some stake in government agencies to marshall resources to fund major infrastructure development projects and accelerate the socio-economic transformation of our Country.

items.title: Kenya: PS Omollo Calls On Boda Boda Operators to Reject Recruitment As Goons-for-Hire, Shun Political Violence
items.blurb: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Principal Secretary for Internal Security and National Administration Raymond Omollo has issued a stern warning against the use of boda boda operators to perpetrate political violence, saying the government will crack down on those recruiting riders into criminal gangs and those who participate in acts of goonism.

items.title: Kenya: Sifuna Dismisses Vote-Split Claims, Urges Opposition Unity Ahead
items.blurb: [Capital FM] Kisii -- Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) Secretary-General Edwin Sifuna has dismissed claims that he or the Linda Mwananchi movement could be used to split the opposition vote ahead of the 2027 General Election.

items.title: Liberia: Judge Throws Out Case Against Vicky Gray, Finds No Evidence Linking Her to Alleged Assault
items.blurb: [FrontPageAfrica] Monrovia -- The Monrovia City Court has dismissed all criminal charges against Dr. Vicky Gray, ruling that prosecutors failed to establish probable cause connecting her to an alleged violent assault at Cigar Bar in Sinkor, while ordering three of her co-defendants to stand trial on lesser offenses.

items.title: Ethiopia: Ethiopian Honored With 'Connectivity Award' At 2026 Aviation &amp; Tourism Ceremony
items.blurb: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopian Airlines has been honored with the prestigious 'Connectivity Award' at the 2026 Aviation &amp; Tourism Award Ceremony hosted by Air Transport News.

items.title: Ethiopia: Prime Minister Abiy Inspects Chano-Chencha Road Project
items.blurb: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed today inspected the ongoing Chano-Chencha Road Project, highlighting infrastructure and tourism as drivers of growth.

items.title: Africa: Hassan Hails Growing African Football Ahead of Australia Clash
items.blurb: [CAF] Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan believes his side's experience against African opposition has prepared them well for Friday's FIFA World Cup Round of 32 meeting with Australia.

items.title: Namibia: Missing Persons More Than a Statistic
items.blurb: [New Era] Namibia has seen a rise in missing persons cases, including those involving minor children and teenagers. The Namibian Police has recorded an annual resolved-and-found rate of roughly 89% to 90%. Recent figures indicate that, over a reporting period of less than a year, 432 missing persons cases were opened. items.title: Uganda: UN Chief Voices Concern Over Political Arrests, Media Freedom in Uganda
items.blurb: [Nile Post] The United Nations Secretary-General, António Guterres, has expressed concern over recent political developments in Uganda, citing reports of the detention of political and civic actors, cases of individuals whose whereabouts remain unknown, and growing concerns over media freedom.

items.title: Kenya: Moi University to Cut Jobs, Save Sh100mn Monthly
items.blurb: [Capital FM] Moi University has revived plans to downsize its workforce after a court allowed it to restart a redundancy process aimed at reducing the institution's ballooning wage bill and restoring its financial stability.

items.title: Ethiopia: Ethiopia's Coffee Export Earnings Reach Historic U.S.$3 Billion Milestone
items.blurb: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia has achieved a historic milestone in its coffee industry, generating a record 3 billion USD in export earnings during the current fiscal year, its highest coffee export revenue on record.
